title: Can action reconstruction make the token editor obey its edit?
decision: Retain LDAD only as a provisional dynamics regularizer, reject every tested coefficient as a primitive causal-action solution, and next isolate corruption distribution and fresh-data exposure with a lower-learning-rate LDAD optimization control.
plain_summary: All five LDAD runs completed and substantially reduced prediction error while preserving rank, but shuffled actions changed error by only 0.6 to 1.1 percent, far below the predeclared 5 percent causal-use gate. LDAD 20 had the best raw error and the largest gradient spike.
